title: Percentage Calculation
explanation: 15% of 240 = (15/100) × 240 = 0.15 × 240 = 36. Choosing 24 is 10% of 240 (only the 10% part). Choosing 30 is 12.5% (half of 25%). Choosing 48 is 20% of 240.

title: Profit Percentage
text: An item costing Rs. 500 is sold for Rs. 625. What is the profit percentage?
explanation: Profit = SP - CP = 625 - 500 = 125. Profit % = (Profit/CP) × 100 = (125/500) × 100 = 25%. Choosing 20% calculates profit as a percentage of SP instead of CP: 125/625 = 20%.

title: Simple Interest
text: What is the simple interest on Rs. 3000 at 8% per annum for 3 years?
explanation: SI = P × R × T / 100 = 3000 × 8 × 3 / 100 = 720. Choosing 600 forgets to multiply by time (only 1 year). Choosing 7200 hasn't divided by 100. Choosing 240 calculates interest for 1 year at 8%.

title: Ratio and Proportion
text: If A : B = 2 : 3 and B : C = 4 : 5, what is A : C?
explanation: A : C = (A/B) × (B/C) = (2/3) × (4/5) = 8/15 = 8 : 15. Choosing 2 : 5 directly multiplies A and C values without considering B. Choosing 4 : 5 is just the second ratio.

title: Discount Problem
text: A shirt marked at Rs. 800 is sold at a 15% discount. What is the selling price?
explanation: Discount = 15% of 800 = 120. SP = 800 - 120 = 680. Choosing 720 is a 10% discount (80 off). Choosing 760 is a 5% discount (40 off).

title: Average Calculation
text: The average of 5 numbers is 24. If one number is removed, the average becomes 22. What is the removed number?
explanation: Sum of 5 numbers = 5 × 24 = 120. Sum of remaining 4 = 4 × 22 = 88. Removed number = 120 - 88 = 32. Choosing 28 comes from incorrectly computing 24 + 4 (the difference in averages).

title: Time and Work
text: If 8 workers can build a wall in 12 days, how many days will 6 workers take to build the same wall? (Assume all workers work at the same rate)
explanation: Total work = 8 × 12 = 96 worker-days. With 6 workers: days = 96/6 = 16. Choosing 9 incorrectly assumes inverse proportion with ratio 6/8 (8 × 6/8 = 9). This is the wrong way to apply the ratio.

title: Compound Interest Comparison
text: The difference between compound interest and simple interest on a sum for 2 years at 10% per annum is Rs. 50. What is the sum?
explanation: CI - SI for 2 years = P(r/100)². Here, 50 = P(10/100)² = P(0.01), so P = 5000. Choosing 4500 comes from a miscalculation of the difference formula.

title: Alligation
text: In what ratio must water be mixed with milk costing Rs. 30 per litre to get a mixture worth Rs. 24 per litre?
explanation: Using alligation: Cost of water = 0, milk = 30, mixture = 24. Ratio = (30-24) : (24-0) = 6 : 24 = 1 : 4 (water : milk). Choosing 1 : 5 or 4 : 1 inverts the ratio or misapplies the alligation rule.

title: Successive Discounts
text: Two successive discounts of 10% and 20% are offered on an item. What is the equivalent single discount?
explanation: Let MP = 100. After 10% discount: 90. After 20% discount on 90: 90 - 18 = 72. Total discount = 28%. The common mistake is to add them: 10% + 20% = 30%. The discounts are applied on different bases.

title: Partnership
text: A and B invest in a business in the ratio 3 : 5. If the total profit is Rs. 16,000, what is A's share?
explanation: A's share = (3/(3+5)) × 16000 = 3/8 × 16000 = 6000. Choosing 8000 assumes equal sharing. Choosing 10000 is B's share (5/8).

title: Clock Angle
text: What is the angle between the hour and minute hands at 3:30?
explanation: At 3:30, the minute hand is at 6 (180° from 12). The hour hand is halfway between 3 and 4 (at 3.5 hours × 30° = 105° from 12). Angle difference = 180° - 105° = 75°. Choosing 90° mistakenly assumes the hour hand stays at 3.

title: Calendar Day
text: If 1st January of a non-leap year is a Monday, what day is 1st March of the same year?
explanation: January has 31 days (3 odd days). So 1st February = Monday + 3 = Thursday. February (non-leap) has 28 days (0 odd days). So 1st March = Thursday + 0 = Thursday.

title: Pipe and Cistern
text: Pipe A can fill a tank in 6 hours. Pipe B can empty it in 8 hours. If both are opened together, how long will it take to fill the tank?
explanation: A's rate = 1/6 tank per hour (filling). B's rate = -1/8 tank per hour (emptying). Combined rate = 1/6 - 1/8 = 4/24 - 3/24 = 1/24. Time = 24 hours. Choosing 14 hours adds the times (6+8). Choosing 7 averages them.

title: Mean of Grouped Data
text: The mean of 10 numbers is 15. If each number is multiplied by 2, what is the new mean?
explanation: If each observation is multiplied by a constant k, the mean is also multiplied by k. Here k = 2, so new mean = 2 × 15 = 30. Choosing 15 assumes the mean doesn't change. Choosing 17 adds 2 instead of multiplying.

title: Loss Percentage
text: An item costing Rs. 400 is sold for Rs. 340. What is the loss percentage?
explanation: Loss = CP - SP = 400 - 340 = 60. Loss % = (60/400) × 100 = 15%. Choosing 10% calculates (60/600) × 100, using a wrong cost base.

title: Speed and Time
text: Walking at 4/5 of his usual speed, a man is 15 minutes late. What is his usual time?
explanation: Let usual time = T minutes. At 4/5 speed, time = (5/4)T. Difference = (5/4)T - T = T/4 = 15 minutes, so T = 60 minutes. Choosing 45 or 75 comes from wrong fraction manipulation.

title: Mixture Replacement
text: From a 20-litre mixture of milk and water in ratio 3 : 1, 4 litres are removed and replaced with water. What is the new ratio of milk to water?
explanation: Initial: milk = 15L, water = 5L. Remove 4L mixture: milk removed = 4 × 3/4 = 3L, water removed = 1L. Remaining: milk = 12L, water = 4L. Add 4L water: milk = 12L, water = 8L. Ratio = 12:8 = 3:2. Choosing 1:1 incorrectly assumes equal removal from each component.

title: Train Crossing Platform
text: A 150 m long train crosses a 250 m long platform in 20 seconds. What is the speed of the train?
explanation: Total distance = train length + platform length = 150 + 250 = 400 m. Time = 20 s. Speed = 400/20 = 20 m/s = 20 × 18/5 = 72 km/h. Choosing 45 km/h uses only the train's length. Choosing 60 km/h uses only the platform's length.
